name: Briami- Greek Vegetable Casserole
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare the vegetables: Cut the eggplant, zucchini and potatoes into bite-sized chunks (do not peel the zucchini or the eggplant). Remove the stems and seeds from the peppers and slice them into strips. Peel and slice the onions. Dice the tomatoes.
2. Saute' the vegetables except the tomatoes in olive oil in small batches. Sauté each batch for 2-3 minutes, then remove from the pan and try to drain some of the oil so that enough oil is left for the next batch. When done, most (if not all) of the oil should be gone from the pan.
3. Place the sauteed vegetables in a baking dish and toss them briefly to ensure even distribution without any vegetable being concentrated in one area.
4. Add the tomatoes into the pan and sauté for a couple of minutes. Crush the garlic and add it to the tomatoes. Add the sugar, salt, and pepper to taste and simmer for another minute.
5. Pour the tomato sauce over the vegetables and bake at 350°F until the vegetables are tender. Serve with plenty of fresh bread and, if desired, some feta cheese on the side.
RATING: Difficulty: easy. Time: 30-45 minutes preparation, 1 hour baking. Precision: No need to measure.
